title: Repatriation
content: Expenses, Advance, Recovery, and Costs. The 11 repatriation expenses that shall be borne by the shipowner or the manning agency 12 concerned shall include: 13 14 (a) Basic pay and allowances from the moment the seafarers leave the ship until 15 they reach the repatriation destination; 16 17 (b) Accommodation and food from the moment the seafarers leave the ship until 18 they reach the repatriation destination; 19 20 (c) Transportation charges, wherein the default mode of transport should be by air; 21 (d) Deployment cost of the shipowner; and 22 23 (e) Immigration fees, fines, and penalties. 24 25 However, in cases where the termination of employment is for just cause, voluntary 26 resignation or upon the request of the seafarer, the cost of repatriation shall be for the 27 account of the seafarer in accordance with the employment agreement. 28 29 The responsibility to provide for the repatriation costs shall be without prejudice to the 30 right to civil, criminal, or other claims in accordance with law or otherwise, particularly in 31 cases of maritime accident or death other than natural causes. 32 33
